untranslatable_reason: This signal requires options-specific indicators not in the allowed set: IV percentile rank, ATM straddle IV rank, implied volatility, realized volatility, spread Greeks (vega, gamma), and time decay (theta). The signal is a meta-rule for selecting volatility spread structures based on IV forecasts and Greeks alignment rather than a price-based technical entry/exit rule. Standard OHLCV indicators cannot replicate IV rank, realized-vs-implied vol comparisons, or Greek exposures.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_75_0 is a framework rule from volatility trading theory (Sheldon Natenberg) describing how to match spread selection to directional and volatility forecasts. It requires: (1) IV rank/percentile calculations, (2) realized volatility measurement, (3) options Greeks (vega/gamma signs), and (4) theta decay tracking. None of these are available in the standard price/volume/momentum indicator set. To implement this signal requires an options analytics layer with IV surface data and Greeks calculations.
